sn35.228:1.1: “‘The ocean, the ocean,’ says the uninstructed run-of-the-mill person.
sn35.228:1.2: But that’s not the ocean in the discipline of the noble ones.
sn35.228:1.3: It’s a great mass of water, a great body of water.
sn35.228:1.5: and its current consists of forms.
sn35.228:2.1: Whoever resists that current consisting of forms is said to have crossed over the ocean of the eye with its waves, whirlpools, sharks, & demons.
sn35.228:2.2: Crossed over, gone beyond, one stands on high ground, a brahman. “The ear is a person’s ocean… “The nose is a person’s ocean…
sn35.228:2.7: “The body is a person’s ocean… “The intellect is a person’s ocean,
sn35.228:2.8: and its current consists of ideas.
sn35.228:2.9: Whoever resists that current consisting of ideas is said to have crossed over the ocean of the intellect with its waves, whirlpools, sharks, & demons.
sn35.228:2.10: Crossed over, gone beyond, one stands on firm ground, a brahman.”
sn35.228:2.11: That is what the Blessed One said. When the One Well-gone had said that, he—
sn35.228:3.1: Whoever crosses over this ocean, with its danger of sharks,
sn35.228:3.2: demons, waves, so very hard to cross is called:
sn35.228:3.3: an attainer of wisdom who has lived the holy life,
sn35.228:3.4: one who’s attained the end of the cosmos, one gone beyond.
